Human-readable name of the volume tier (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum).
Current lifecycle status of the volume tier threshold configuration.
Type of transaction this tier applies to (purchase, refund, cash advance, balance inquiry). Null indicates tier applies to all transaction types.
Date and time when this volume tier threshold record was last modified.
Maximum volume threshold value for this tier (exclusive). Null indicates no upper limit.
Version number of this tier configuration, incremented with each modification to support change tracking and audit requirements.

business_glossary_term: Bank Identification Number (BIN) Interchange Rule ID
description: Unique surrogate primary key identifying a BIN-level interchange qualification rule record in the silver layer lakehouse. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a configuration/rule resource governing interchange qualification for a BIN range.
Reference to the issuing bank or financial institution that issued the card associated with this BIN range. The issuing bank is the cardholder's bank in the payment chain.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Federal Reserve Regulation II

description: Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: bin_interchange_rule references IRF category; adds irf_rate_category_id FK
Reference to the card scheme (payment network brand) governing this interchange rule, such as Visa, Mastercard, American Express, or Discover. Links to the scheme reference product.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; EMVCo

description: Reference to the bin_interchange_rule_id of the newer rule that supersedes this record when rule_status is 'superseded'. Enables rule lineage tracking and historical interchange qualification reconstruction.
reference: SOX; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Specifies the type of authorization associated with this interchange rule: pre-authorization (hotel/car rental), final authorization, or incremental authorization. Different authorization types may qualify for distinct interchange rates.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; ISO 8583

description: Indicates whether Address Verification System (AVS) check is required for a transaction to qualify for this interchange rate category. AVS validation is a fraud prevention measure commonly required for card-not-present interchange qualification.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; PCI DSS v4.0

description: The ending BIN (Bank Identification Number) of the BIN range to which this interchange qualification rule applies. Together with bin_range_start, defines the full BIN range covered by this rule.
reference: ISO/IEC 7812; PCI DSS v4.0; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: The starting BIN (Bank Identification Number), also known as IIN (Issuer Identification Number), of the BIN range to which this interchange qualification rule applies. Supports 6-digit legacy BINs and 8-digit extended BINs per ISO/IEC 7812.
reference: ISO/IEC 7812; PCI DSS v4.0; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: The payment network brand associated with this BIN range (e.g., VISA, MASTERCARD). Used in conjunction with scheme_id for interchange qualification and reporting.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; EMVCo

description: Indicates whether this interchange rule applies to card-not-present (CNP) transactions, such as e-commerce, mail order, or telephone order (MOTO) transactions. CNP transactions typically carry higher interchange rates due to elevated fraud risk.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Indicates whether this interchange rule applies to card-present (CP) transactions, where the physical card or device is used at a POS or mPOS terminal. Card-present transactions typically qualify for lower interchange rates than card-not-present.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; EMVCo EMV Chip Specifications

description: Classification of the card product associated with this BIN range, determining the applicable interchange rate tier. Debit and prepaid cards may be subject to Durbin Amendment regulated caps; commercial and government cards follow separate interchange schedules. Serves as the primary CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: debit|credit|prepaid|commercial|government|healthcare — promote to reference product]
reference: Federal Reserve Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; ISO 7816

description: Indicates whether this interchange rule applies to contactless NFC (Near Field Communication) transactions, including tap-to-pay and mobile wallet payments. Contactless qualification may carry distinct interchange rates from contact chip transactions.
reference: EMVCo Contactless Specifications; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; NFC Forum

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the issuing country associated with this BIN range (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Determines domestic vs. cross-border interchange rate applicability and regulatory jurisdiction.
reference: ISO 3166-1; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; GDPR; PSD2

description: Timestamp when this BIN interchange rule record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for audit trail and data lineage purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX; GDPR Article 5; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Indicates whether this interchange rule applies specifically to cross-border transactions where the issuing country differs from the acquiring country. Cross-border transactions typically attract higher interchange rates and additional scheme fees.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; PSD2; SWIFT

description: Indicates the level of transaction data (Level 1, Level 2, Level 3) required for interchange qualification under this rule. Level 2/3 data (enhanced transaction detail for B2B/commercial cards) may qualify for lower interchange rates.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Reason code explaining why a BIN range is exempt from Durbin Amendment regulated interchange caps. Applicable only when durbin_regulated_flag is False. Small issuers (assets < $10B), government-administered programs, and certain prepaid products qualify for exemption.
reference: Federal Reserve Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Dodd-Frank Act Section 1075; CFPB

description: Indicates whether this BIN range is subject to the Durbin Amendment interchange fee caps under Federal Reserve Regulation II. True = regulated (issuer assets ≥ $10B threshold, capped at 21 cents + 0.05% + 1 cent fraud adjustment); False = unregulated (exempt issuers, higher interchange applies).
reference: Federal Reserve Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Dodd-Frank Act Section 1075

description: The date on which this BIN interchange qualification rule ceases to be effective. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no defined expiry. Used to manage rule versioning and historical interchange cost analysis. Serves as EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Federal Reserve Regulation II

description: The date from which this BIN interchange qualification rule becomes effective and is applied during transaction processing and interchange qualification. Aligns with scheme fee schedule publication cycles (typically semi-annual for Visa/Mastercard). Serves as EFFECTIVE_FROM for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Federal Reserve Regulation II

description: Indicates whether EMV (Europay Mastercard Visa) chip processing is required for a transaction to qualify for this interchange rate category. EMV chip transactions reduce fraud liability and may qualify for preferential interchange rates.
reference: EMVCo EMV Chip Specifications; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Indicates whether this interchange rule applies to installment payment transactions, including BNPL (Buy Now Pay Later) arrangements. Installment transactions may carry distinct interchange qualification criteria.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; CFPB

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the interchange fixed fee is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for cross-border interchange cost calculations and multi-currency acquiring portfolios.
reference: ISO 4217; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: The fixed per-transaction fee component of the interchange, expressed in the applicable currency (e.g., 0.1000 = $0.10 per transaction). Combined with interchange_rate_percent to compute total interchange cost per transaction.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Federal Reserve Regulation II

description: The percentage-based component of the interchange fee applied to the transaction amount for qualifying transactions under this BIN rule (e.g., 1.6500 represents 1.65%). Used in cost-of-acceptance calculations for acquiring portfolios. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Federal Reserve Regulation II; SOX

description: Comma-separated list of MCC (Merchant Category Code) values that are included or excluded from this interchange rule, as determined by mcc_restriction_type. For example, '5411,5412,5499' for grocery MCCs. Null when mcc_restriction_type is 'all'.
reference: ISO 18245 (MCC Standard); Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Defines how MCC (Merchant Category Code) restrictions apply to this interchange rule: 'all' means no MCC restriction (applies to all MCCs), 'include' means only specified MCCs qualify, 'exclude' means specified MCCs are excluded, 'none' means no transactions qualify regardless of MCC.
reference: ISO 18245 (MCC Standard); Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Indicates whether this interchange rule applies to recurring or subscription-based transactions. Recurring transactions may qualify for specific interchange programs with distinct rate structures.

Card scheme-defined geographic region code associated with this BIN range (e.g., 'AP' for Asia Pacific, 'CEMEA' for Central Europe Middle East Africa, 'LAC' for Latin America and Caribbean, 'NA' for North America). Used for cross-border interchange rate determination.

Externally-known unique business code identifying this BIN interchange qualification rule, used in downstream transaction processing and interchange qualification engines. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; ISO 8583

description: Free-text description providing additional context about this BIN interchange qualification rule, including any special conditions, exceptions, or notes from the card scheme or compliance team.
Human-readable descriptive name for the BIN interchange qualification rule (e.g., 'Visa Debit Regulated CPS Retail'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.

Current lifecycle status of this BIN interchange qualification rule. Active rules are applied in real-time interchange qualification; superseded rules have been replaced by newer versions; expired rules are past their effective end date. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; SOX

description: Card scheme-assigned program code identifying the specific interchange program under which this rule is classified (e.g., 'CPS/RETAIL', 'EIRF', 'MERIT III', 'WORLD', 'SIGNATURE'). Used for interchange qualification matching in the transaction processing platform.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; ISO 8583

description: The native rule identifier or reference code from the originating operational system (Transaction Processing Platform or Payment Gateway) from which this BIN interchange rule was sourced. Supports data lineage and reconciliation back to the system of record.

Indicates whether 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication is required for card-not-present transactions to qualify for this interchange rate. 3DS-authenticated transactions may qualify for lower interchange rates and shift fraud liability to the issuer. Relevant under PSD2 SCA requirements.
reference: EMVCo 3-D Secure Specification; PSD2 Article 97 (SCA); Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Indicates whether transactions using a DPAN (Device Primary Account Number) or network token issued by a TSP (Token Service Provider) for this BIN range are eligible for this interchange rate. Tokenized transactions may qualify for distinct interchange rates.
reference: EMVCo Tokenization Framework; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Maximum transaction amount (in the fee currency) eligible for this interchange rate category. Transactions exceeding this threshold may be subject to different interchange rates or require additional qualification criteria.

Minimum transaction amount (in the fee currency) required for a transaction to qualify for this interchange rate category. Transactions below this threshold may downgrade to a less favorable interchange tier.

Timestamp when this BIN interchange rule record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, rule versioning audit, and regulatory compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX; GDPR Article 5; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Sequential version number of this BIN interchange rule record, incremented each time the rule is updated. Supports rule versioning and historical interchange qualification audit trails required for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution.
reference: SOX; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each Durbin Amendment (Regulation II) compliance tier record in the registry. Primary key for the durbin_compliance_tier data product.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment)

description: Indicates whether issuers in this compliance tier are subject to annual Federal Reserve data collection and review (e.g., the Federal Reserve's annual debit card survey under Regulation II). Regulated issuers above the $10 billion threshold are subject to annual reporting.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Federal Reserve Board Debit Card Survey

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the geographic jurisdiction in which this compliance tier applies. Regulation II is a US domestic regulation; this field is 'USA' for all standard Durbin tiers but supports future multi-jurisdiction extensions.
reference: ISO 3166-1 (Country Codes); Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment)

description: Consolidated asset threshold in US dollars used to determine whether an issuer is subject to Regulation II interchange caps. The Federal Reserve sets this at $10 billion; issuers at or above this threshold are regulated. Stored as the threshold value applicable to this tier.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR 235.5; Federal Reserve Board

description: Ending Bank Identification Number (BIN) / Issuer Identification Number (IIN) of the BIN range to which this compliance tier applies. Used in conjunction with bin_range_start to define the full BIN range scope for interchange qualification.
reference: ISO 7812 (Identification of Issuers); Federal Reserve System – Regulation II; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Starting Bank Identification Number (BIN) / Issuer Identification Number (IIN) of the BIN range to which this compliance tier applies. Enables BIN-level interchange qualification routing. Stored as a 6–8 digit string per ISO 7812.
reference: ISO 7812 (Identification of Issuers); Federal Reserve System – Regulation II; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Payment network brand (card scheme) to which this compliance tier applies. Regulation II applies to all debit card networks; however, interchange cap and routing rules may differ by scheme. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visa|mastercard|discover|amex|interlink|star|pulse|nyce|maestro|unionpay — promote to reference product]
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; NACHA

description: Name or identifier of the internal compliance team or officer responsible for maintaining and certifying this compliance tier record. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements under SOX and internal governance frameworks.
reference: SOX – Sarbanes-Oxley Act; Internal Compliance Governance; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Indicates the directional impact of this compliance tier on merchant cost of acceptance (MDR/MSF). Regulated tiers with capped interchange typically reduce cost of acceptance for merchants; exempt tiers may result in higher interchange and increased merchant cost.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Timestamp when this compliance tier record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit creation trail required for SOX compliance and data lineage in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: SOX – Sarbanes-Oxley Act; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the currency in which interchange caps and fee amounts in this compliance tier are denominated. Typically 'USD' for Regulation II tiers.
reference: ISO 4217 (Currency Codes); Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment)

description: Name of the operational system of record from which this compliance tier record originates or is maintained (e.g., 'Risk and Compliance Management System', 'Transaction Processing Platform'). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: Data Warehouse and Analytics Platform; Risk and Compliance Management System

description: Type of debit card program covered by this compliance tier. Regulation II exemptions differ by card type — government-administered and certain reloadable prepaid programs are exempt from interchange caps. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: consumer_debit|prepaid|government_benefit|payroll|healthcare_fsa|commercial_debit — promote to reference product]
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR 235.5; CFPB

description: Calendar date on which this Durbin compliance tier configuration becomes effective and applicable to interchange cap enforcement and routing compliance checks. Aligns with Federal Reserve regulatory reporting periods.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Federal Reserve Board

description: Indicates whether EMV (Europay Mastercard Visa) chip card technology is required for transactions qualifying under this compliance tier. Relevant for fraud-adjustment eligibility under Regulation II, which requires issuers to implement chip technology as part of fraud-prevention standards.
reference: EMVCo EMV Chip Specifications; Federal Reserve System – Regulation II 12 CFR 235.4; PCI DSS

description: Number of unaffiliated payment networks on which debit cards in this compliance tier are enabled for routing. Regulation II requires a minimum of two unaffiliated networks. Used to validate network exclusivity compliance.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR 235.7; Federal Reserve Board

description: Calendar date on which this Durbin compliance tier configuration ceases to be effective. Null for open-ended tiers that remain in force until superseded. Used in date-range queries for point-in-time compliance reporting.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Federal Reserve Board

description: Specific Federal Register or Code of Federal Regulations citation underpinning this compliance tier (e.g., '12 CFR Part 235', 'Federal Register Vol. 76 No. 130'). Provides a direct regulatory audit trail for compliance and legal review.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Federal Register

description: Federal Reserve reporting period to which this compliance tier applies, formatted as YYYY-H1, YYYY-H2, YYYY-Q1, etc. The Federal Reserve collects debit card interchange data annually; this field links the tier to the applicable regulatory data collection cycle.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Federal Reserve Board Debit Card Survey

description: Maximum additional interchange allowance in US cents per transaction for fraud-prevention activities, available to eligible regulated issuers under Regulation II. Currently set at 1 cent per transaction by the Federal Reserve. Null if fraud_adjustment_eligible is false.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR 235.4; Federal Reserve Board

description: Indicates whether issuers in this compliance tier are eligible to receive the 1-cent fraud-prevention adjustment allowance on top of the base interchange cap under Regulation II. Eligibility requires the issuer to meet the Federal Reserve's fraud-prevention standards annually.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR 235.4; Federal Reserve Board

business_glossary_term: Interchange Cap Ad Valorem Rate (Basis Points)
description: Ad valorem (percentage-based) component of the Regulation II interchange cap expressed in basis points of the transaction value. Under the Federal Reserve rule, regulated issuers may charge up to 0.05% (5 basis points) of the transaction amount in addition to the fixed cent cap.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR 235.3; Federal Reserve Board

business_glossary_term: Interchange Reimbursement Fee (IRF) Cap (Cents per Transaction)
description: Maximum allowable interchange fee in US cents per debit card transaction under Regulation II for this compliance tier. For regulated issuers, the Federal Reserve base cap is 21 cents plus 0.05% of the transaction value, with an optional 1-cent fraud-adjustment allowance. This field stores the fixed per-transaction cent component of the cap.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR 235.3; Federal Reserve Board

description: Categorical asset size band of the issuer population covered by this tier, used for segmentation and analytics. 'above_10b' corresponds to regulated issuers under Regulation II; bands below $10 billion are exempt. Supports revenue and cost-of-acceptance analysis by issuer size.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR 235.5; Federal Reserve Board

description: Classification of the issuer under Regulation II based on asset threshold and card program type. 'regulated' applies to issuers with consolidated assets ≥ $10 billion; 'exempt' covers small issuers below the threshold; 'government_administered' and 'reloadable_prepaid' reflect statutory exemptions under Regulation II Section 235.5.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR Part 235; CFPB

description: Date on which this compliance tier was last reviewed against current Federal Reserve Regulation II rules and issuer asset threshold data. Used to track compliance review cadence and identify tiers due for reassessment.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Internal Compliance Governance

description: Indicates whether merchants are permitted to exercise least-cost routing (LCR) or preferred network routing for transactions under this compliance tier, as required by Regulation II. True = merchant routing choice is enabled and enforced; False = routing is issuer-controlled.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR 235.7; Federal Reserve Board

description: Indicates whether issuers in this tier comply with the Regulation II network exclusivity prohibition, which requires that each debit card be enabled on at least two unaffiliated payment networks (e.g., Visa and a PIN debit network). True = compliant; False = non-compliant or under review.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR 235.7; Federal Reserve Board

description: Scheduled date for the next compliance review of this tier against Federal Reserve Regulation II requirements and updated issuer asset threshold data. Supports proactive compliance management and audit scheduling.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Internal Compliance Governance

description: Free-text field for additional regulatory context, internal compliance annotations, or exception documentation related to this compliance tier. Used by compliance officers to record interpretive guidance or Federal Reserve correspondence.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Internal Compliance Governance

description: Indicates whether PIN-authenticated debit routing must be offered as one of the two unaffiliated network options under Regulation II network exclusivity requirements for this compliance tier. Supports routing compliance validation.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) 12 CFR 235.7; Federal Reserve Board

description: Classifies the financial impact of this compliance tier on the acquiring or issuing portfolio. 'revenue_generating' = tier enables interchange income above cost; 'cost_center' = tier results in net interchange cost; 'neutral' = break-even. Supports revenue recognition and cost-of-acceptance analysis in ERP – Oracle Financials.
reference: ERP – Oracle Financials (Revenue Recognition); SOX – Sarbanes-Oxley Act; Federal Reserve System – Regulation II

description: Indicates whether this compliance tier applies to dual-message (signature debit, e.g., Visa/Mastercard), single-message (PIN debit, e.g., Interlink/STAR), or both routing architectures. Dual-message transactions are authorized and cleared separately; single-message transactions are authorized and cleared in one message per ISO 8583.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); ISO 8583; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Tier code of the compliance tier record that replaced this record when it was superseded. Null if this tier is currently active or was retired without a direct successor. Enables forward-chaining of tier version history for regulatory audit trails.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); SOX – Sarbanes-Oxley Act

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this compliance tier configuration (e.g., REG_TIER_1, EXEMPT_TIER_A). Used as the business-facing identifier in regulatory filings and interchange routing tables.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); ISO 8583

description: Detailed narrative description of the compliance tier, including the regulatory basis, applicable issuer population, and interchange cap rationale. Supports audit and regulatory review.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment)

description: Human-readable name describing the compliance tier (e.g., 'Regulated Issuer – Standard Cap', 'Small Issuer Exempt – Fraud Adjustment Eligible'). Used in reporting dashboards and regulatory documentation.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment)

description: Current lifecycle status of this compliance tier record. 'active' = currently in force; 'inactive' = no longer applicable; 'pending' = approved but not yet effective; 'superseded' = replaced by a newer tier version; 'under_review' = subject to Federal Reserve or internal compliance review.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Internal Compliance Governance

description: Payment transaction channel to which this compliance tier's interchange cap and routing rules apply. Regulation II routing requirements may differ between card-present (POS/NFC) and card-not-present (e-commerce) environments.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; ISO 8583

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this compliance tier record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to detect stale configurations and support incremental ETL processing in the Data Warehouse and Analytics Platform.
reference: SOX – Sarbanes-Oxley Act; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Monotonically incrementing version number for this compliance tier record, incremented each time the tier configuration is updated (e.g., cap amount change, routing rule update). Supports point-in-time compliance auditing and change history tracking.
reference: Federal Reserve System – Regulation II (Durbin Amendment); SOX – Sarbanes-Oxley Act

business_glossary_term: Interchange Reimbursement Fee (IRF) Rate Category ID
description: Unique identifier for the interchange rate category. Primary key.
Reference to the card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) that defines this interchange rate category.
The maximum number of hours between authorization and capture for transactions to qualify for this interchange rate category.
Indicates whether Address Verification System (AVS) validation is required to qualify for this interchange rate category.
The ending Bank Identification Number (BIN) or Issuer Identification Number (IIN) in the range of card products that qualify for this interchange rate category.
The starting Bank Identification Number (BIN) or Issuer Identification Number (IIN) in the range of card products that qualify for this interchange rate category.
Indicates whether this interchange rate category applies to card-not-present (CNP) transactions such as e-commerce, mail order, or telephone order.
Indicates whether this interchange rate category applies to card-present (CP) transactions where the physical card is presented at the Point of Sale (POS).
The unique code assigned by the card scheme to identify this interchange rate category (e.g., CPS_RETAIL, MERIT_III, OPTBLUE_TIER1).
The full business name of the interchange rate category as published by the card scheme (e.g., Visa CPS/Retail, Mastercard Merit III, Amex OptBlue Standard).
reference: Visa Product and Service Rules, Mastercard Standards

description: The classification of the interchange rate category based on card product type and transaction characteristics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|premium|commercial|debit|credit|prepaid|rewards|government|healthcare — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether contactless NFC transactions are eligible for this interchange rate category.
The timestamp when this interchange rate category record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether this interchange rate category applies to cross-border transactions where the acquiring and issuing banks are in different countries.

business_glossary_term: International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 4217 Currency Code
description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount thresholds and interchange rates associated with this category.
Indicates whether Card Verification Value (CVV) validation is required to qualify for this interchange rate category.
Indicates whether this interchange rate category applies to domestic transactions where the acquiring and issuing banks are in the same country.
The interchange rate category code to which a transaction will be downgraded if it fails to meet the qualification criteria for this category.
A detailed textual description of the conditions under which a transaction will be downgraded from this interchange rate category to a lower-tier category.
Indicates whether this interchange rate category is subject to Durbin Amendment (Regulation II) caps for debit card transactions issued by regulated financial institutions.
The date on which this interchange rate category becomes active and applicable to transactions.
Indicates whether EMV chip authentication is required for transactions to qualify for this interchange rate category.
The date on which this interchange rate category ceases to be active and is no longer applicable to new transactions.
The current lifecycle status of this interchange rate category within the card schemes published rate structure.
The ending Merchant Category Code (MCC) in the range of merchant types that qualify for this interchange rate category.
The starting Merchant Category Code (MCC) in the range of merchant types that qualify for this interchange rate category.
Free-form internal notes and comments about this interchange rate category for operational reference and business context.
The code of the parent or umbrella interchange rate category under which this category is nested in the schemes taxonomy.
The priority ranking used to determine which interchange rate category applies when a transaction qualifies for multiple categories. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.
A detailed textual description of all the business and technical criteria that a transaction must meet to qualify for this interchange rate category.
reference: Visa Product and Service Rules, Mastercard Standards

description: The three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code indicating the geographic scope where this interchange rate category applies.
The reference identifier or document name from the card schemes official publication where this interchange rate category is defined (e.g., Visa USA Interchange Reimbursement Fees April 2024).
reference: Visa Product and Service Rules, Mastercard Standards

description: The number of days within which transactions in this interchange rate category must be settled to qualify for the published interchange rate.
Indicates whether 3-D Secure authentication is required for card-not-present transactions to qualify for this interchange rate category.
The maximum transaction amount allowed to qualify for this interchange rate category, in the base currency of the scheme.
The minimum transaction amount required to qualify for this interchange rate category, in the base currency of the scheme.
The timestamp when this interchange rate category record was last modified in the system.
Unique identifier for the interchange reimbursement fee table record.
Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Card scheme lookup drives scheme‑specific interchange rates and compliance checks.
Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: irf_table references its category; adds irf_rate_category_id FK to irf_rate_category
Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requires mapping issuer ISO country codes to country reference for compliance audits.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the acquiring bank. Used for domestic vs. cross-border interchange rate determination. Null indicates rate applies regardless of acquirer country.
reference: ISO 3166-1; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Card authorization method required for this interchange rate (chip, contactless, magnetic stripe, manual entry, ecommerce, token). Null indicates no specific method requirement.
reference: EMVCo; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Upper bound of the BIN/IIN range to which this interchange rate applies. Used for BIN-level interchange qualification rules. Null indicates no BIN restriction.
reference: ISO/IEC 7812; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Lower bound of the BIN/IIN range to which this interchange rate applies. Used for BIN-level interchange qualification rules. Null indicates no BIN restriction.
reference: ISO/IEC 7812; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Card product tier or brand level (e.g., Standard, Rewards, Premium, Signature, World, World Elite, Platinum, Infinite). Higher tiers typically carry higher interchange rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|rewards|premium|signature|world|world_elite|platinum|infinite — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Type of payment card to which this interchange rate applies (credit, debit, prepaid, commercial, purchasing). Critical qualifier for rate selection.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Regulation II Durbin Amendment

description: Internal classification category used for cost-of-acceptance reporting and analysis (e.g., low-cost, standard, premium, high-cost). Supports merchant pricing strategy and portfolio profitability analysis.
Maximum interchange fee amount allowed under Regulation II Durbin Amendment for regulated debit transactions. Null if not subject to Durbin caps.
reference: Regulation II Durbin Amendment; Federal Reserve System

description: Indicates whether this interchange rate is subject to Regulation II Durbin Amendment caps (True for regulated debit, False otherwise). Critical for US debit interchange compliance.

The start date of the settlement period covered by this cost of acceptance record.
Total Interchange Reimbursement Fee (IRF) paid to issuing banks for all transactions during the settlement period.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Regulation II Durbin Amendment

description: Total Merchant Service Fee (MSF) revenue collected from the merchant for payment processing services during the settlement period.
Total assessment fees charged by card schemes (Visa, Mastercard, etc.) for network usage during the settlement period.
Total number of transactions processed for the merchant during the settlement period.
Timestamp when this cost of acceptance record was last modified.
Unique identifier for the interchange downgrade event.
Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: During dispute resolution, downgrade details are needed to assess cost impact; linking downgrade to dispute case enables that process.
Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: interchange_downgrade references both original and downgraded IRF categories; adds irf_rate_category_id FK
Unique identifier for the merchant whose transaction was downgraded.
Foreign key linking to risk.risk_case. Business justification: Each downgrade may trigger a risk investigation; linking allows auditors to trace downgrade to its risk case.
Reference to the transaction that experienced the interchange downgrade.
Additional reference data provided by the acquiring bank for tracking and reconciliation purposes.
Unique 23-digit reference number assigned by the acquiring bank to track the transaction across the payment network.
The precise date and time when the transaction was authorized by the issuing bank.
Code returned by the issuer indicating the result of address verification, which is often required for optimal interchange qualification.
First 6-8 digits of the card number identifying the issuing bank and card product type.
The precise date and time when the authorized transaction was captured for settlement.
Flag indicating whether the transaction was card-present (true) or card-not-present (false), which affects interchange qualification.
Payment network brand that processed the transaction and applied the downgrade.
The card product tier or level (e.g., standard, rewards, premium, corporate) which influences interchange rates.
Classification of the payment card used in the transaction, which determines applicable interchange rate tables.
The date and time when this downgrade record was first created in the data platform.
Code returned by the issuer indicating the result of CVV verification, which is often required for optimal interchange qualification in card-not-present transactions.
The date and time when the interchange downgrade was identified and recorded in the system.

business_glossary_term: Downgraded Interchange Reimbursement Fee (IRF) Rate in Basis Points
description: The actual interchange rate in basis points applied after the downgrade, representing a higher cost to the merchant.
Flag indicating whether the transaction is subject to Durbin Amendment interchange rate caps for regulated debit cards.
Flag indicating whether the transaction used EMV chip technology, which typically qualifies for lower interchange rates.
The additional interchange fee cost incurred by the merchant due to the downgrade, calculated as the rate differential applied to the transaction amount.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the incremental cost amount.
Four-digit code classifying the merchant's type of business, which influences interchange qualification rules.
The channel through which the transaction was processed, affecting interchange qualification criteria.

business_glossary_term: Original Interchange Reimbursement Fee (IRF) Rate in Basis Points
description: The optimal interchange rate in basis points that would have applied if the transaction had not been downgraded.
Indicates whether the downgrade could have been prevented through merchant action or system configuration changes.
The difference in basis points between the downgraded rate and the original qualified rate, representing the incremental cost penalty.
Standardized code indicating the specific reason for the interchange downgrade, such as missing AVS data, late settlement, or incomplete transaction data.
Human-readable explanation of why the transaction was downgraded, providing context for merchant coaching and remediation.
Suggested corrective action for the merchant to prevent future downgrades of this type, used for merchant coaching and optimization.
The date on which the transaction was settled and the downgrade was applied during clearing.
Number of days between authorization and settlement, where excessive delay can trigger interchange downgrades.
Status of 3-D Secure authentication for the transaction, which can affect interchange qualification for e-commerce transactions.
The gross transaction amount in the transaction currency on which the interchange fees are calculated.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount.
The date on which the original transaction was authorized or processed.
The date and time when this downgrade record was last modified in the data platform.
Unique identifier for the interchange dispute record. Primary key for the interchange dispute entity.
Reference to the interchange billing period that is the subject of the dispute, if the dispute pertains to aggregated billing rather than a single transaction.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_compliance_case. Business justification: Interchange disputes are investigated as compliance cases; linking provides case tracking, resolution status, and auditability.
Reference to the specific transaction that is the subject of the interchange dispute, if the dispute pertains to a single transaction.
Reference to the acquiring bank or merchant acquirer involved in the interchange dispute.
Reference to the issuing bank or card issuer involved in the interchange dispute.
Reference to the merchant associated with the disputed transaction or billing period.
Reference to the card scheme entity involved in the interchange dispute.
Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_invoice. Business justification: Interchange disputes are linked to the underlying scheme invoice; adds scheme_invoice_id FK
Unique acquirer reference number for the disputed transaction, used for tracing and reconciliation across the payment network.
reference: ISO 8583, Visa Core Rules, Mastercard Rules

description: End date of the billing period covered by the interchange dispute, applicable when the dispute relates to aggregated billing.
Start date of the billing period covered by the interchange dispute, applicable when the dispute relates to aggregated billing.
The first 6-8 digits of the card number (BIN or IIN) associated with the disputed transaction, used for interchange qualification.
reference: ISO 8583, Visa Core Rules, Mastercard Rules

description: Payment network brand or card scheme involved in the interchange dispute.
reference: Visa Core Rules, Mastercard Rules, EMVCo

description: Interchange fee amount claimed by the disputing party as the correct amount that should have been applied.

business_glossary_term: Claimed Interchange Reimbursement Fee (IRF) Category Code
description: The interchange rate category code that the disputing party claims should have been applied to the transaction or billing period.
Timestamp when the interchange dispute record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether the disputed transaction involved cross-border processing, which affects interchange qualification.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in the interchange dispute.
Current lifecycle status of the interchange dispute in the resolution workflow.
Classification of the interchange dispute based on the nature of the disagreement.
The difference between the claimed and scheme-reported interchange amounts, representing the financial value in dispute.
Indicates whether the disputed transaction or billing period is subject to Durbin Amendment interchange fee caps for regulated debit cards.
reference: Regulation II Durbin Amendment, Federal Reserve System

description: Current escalation level of the interchange dispute within the card scheme dispute resolution hierarchy.
Date when the interchange dispute was formally filed with the card scheme or counterparty.
Precise timestamp when the interchange dispute was submitted into the dispute management system.
The final financial adjustment amount resulting from the dispute resolution, which may be positive (credit) or negative (debit).
Identifies which party initiated the interchange dispute (acquirer, issuer, payment platform, or card scheme).
Four-digit merchant category code associated with the disputed transaction, relevant for interchange qualification rules.
reference: ISO 18245, Visa Core Rules, Mastercard Rules

description: Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or internal observations related to the interchange dispute.
Standardized code identifying the specific reason for the interchange dispute, aligned with card scheme dispute reason taxonomies.
reference: Visa Core Rules, Mastercard Rules, ISO 8583

description: Detailed textual explanation of the reason for the interchange dispute, providing context beyond the reason code.
External business identifier for the interchange dispute, used for communication with card schemes, acquirers, and issuers.
Date when the interchange dispute was resolved or closed, marking the end of the dispute lifecycle.
Final outcome of the interchange dispute resolution process, indicating whether the dispute was upheld, denied, or partially resolved.
Precise timestamp when the interchange dispute resolution was recorded in the system.
Identifies which party is responding to or defending against the interchange dispute.
Deadline by which the respondent must provide a response to the interchange dispute, per card scheme rules.

business_glossary_term: Scheme Applied Interchange Reimbursement Fee (IRF) Category Code
description: The interchange rate category code that was actually applied by the card scheme, which is being disputed.
Case or dispute tracking number assigned by the card scheme for this interchange dispute.
Interchange fee amount reported or calculated by the card scheme, which is being disputed.
Reference identifier or location of supporting documentation, evidence, or attachments related to the interchange dispute.
Timestamp when the interchange dispute record was last modified in the system.
Unique identifier for the interchange reconciliation record. Primary key.
Reference to the acquiring bank (merchant's bank) involved in the interchange transactions.

business_glossary_term: Acquiring Bank Legal Entity Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Reconciliation reports must reference the acquiring bank's legal entity to resolve variance and audit discrepancies.
Reference to the supervisor or manager who approved the reconciliation results and any adjustments.
Reference to the dispute case opened with the card scheme if the variance could not be internally resolved.
Reference to the user or analyst who performed the reconciliation and investigation.
Reference to the issuing bank (cardholder's bank) involved in the interchange transactions.
Reference to the acquiring bank (merchant's bank) involved in the interchange transactions.
Reference to the user or analyst who performed the reconciliation and investigation.
Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_invoice. Business justification: Interchange reconciliation aligns with a scheme invoice; adds scheme_invoice_id FK
Reference to the settlement period during which the interchange transactions occurred.
Any adjustment amount applied to reconcile the variance, either credited or debited based on dispute resolution.
Standardized code indicating the reason for any adjustment applied to this reconciliation.
The date and time when the reconciliation was approved by the authorized supervisor.
The date and time when this reconciliation record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this reconciliation record.
Identifier for the reconciliation cycle or batch run during which this record was processed.
The date on which a formal dispute was filed with the card scheme regarding this reconciliation variance.
The date on which the scheme dispute was resolved and final settlement amounts were agreed.
The general ledger account code to which interchange reconciliation amounts are posted.
The date on which the reconciliation results and any adjustments were posted to the general ledger.
The total interchange amount calculated internally based on qualification rules and transaction data for the settlement period.
The date on which internal settlement processing was completed for this period.
The date and time when the reconciliation was completed and status was finalized.
Current status of the reconciliation record indicating whether internal and scheme amounts are aligned.
Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this reconciliation record for tracking and audit purposes.
Detailed notes documenting the investigation, resolution actions taken, and final outcome for this reconciliation record.
The date on which revenue or cost associated with this interchange reconciliation is recognized for financial reporting.
High-level categorization of the root cause identified for any variance in this reconciliation.
Detailed explanation of the root cause identified for the variance, including specific transaction or rule issues.
The payment card scheme (network brand) for which this reconciliation is performed.
The total interchange amount reported by the card scheme for the same settlement period.
The date on which the card scheme settled the interchange amounts.
The end date of the settlement period covered by this reconciliation.
The start date of the settlement period covered by this reconciliation.
The total number of transactions included in the internal interchange calculation for this period.
The total number of transactions reported by the card scheme for this period.
The difference in transaction counts between internal records and scheme reports.
The date and time when this reconciliation record was last modified.
The absolute difference between internal calculated interchange and scheme reported interchange (internal minus scheme).
The variance expressed as a percentage of the scheme reported interchange amount.
The absolute monetary threshold above which variances must be investigated and resolved.
Indicates whether the variance amount or percentage exceeds the acceptable tolerance threshold requiring investigation.
The percentage threshold above which variances must be investigated and resolved.
Unique identifier for the interchange qualification record. Primary key for this transactional record of interchange rate qualification outcome.
Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Qualification logic depends on card‑scheme specific rules stored in reference.
Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: interchange_qualification references qualified IRF category; adds irf_rate_category_id FK
Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: MCC determines eligibility and rate tiers for interchange qualification.
Reference to the merchant who accepted the transaction. Links to merchant management system for merchant-level interchange analysis and cost-of-acceptance reporting.
Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Regulatory interchange fee reporting requires the FX rate used to convert foreign‑currency transaction amounts to settlement currency.
Foreign key linking to risk.risk_profile. Business justification: Pricing reports need merchant risk tier; linking qualification to risk_profile enables tier‑based fee calculations.
Foreign key linking to settlement.cycle. Business justification: Enables qualification data to be associated with the settlement cycle used in interchange fee calculations and audit trails.
Reference to the cleared transaction for which interchange qualification was performed. Links to the transaction processing platform record.
Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Transaction type is required for rule‑based qualification and reporting.
Unique 23-digit acquirer reference number assigned to the transaction by the acquiring bank. Used for transaction tracing across the payment network.
Date on which the transaction was authorized. Time between authorization and settlement is a factor in interchange qualification.
Result code from address verification system check performed during authorization. AVS match is a qualifying criterion for many card-not-present interchange categories.

business_glossary_term: Bank Identification Number (BIN) / Issuer Identification Number (IIN)
description: First 6-8 digits of the card number identifying the issuing bank and card program. BIN-level interchange qualification rules determine which IRF category applies based on issuer and card product.
Indicates whether the transaction was card-present (face-to-face) or card-not-present (e-commerce, mail order, phone order). Card-present transactions typically qualify for lower interchange rates.
Type of payment card used in the transaction. Card type is a primary factor in interchange qualification as different card types have different IRF schedules.
reference: Visa Product and Service Rules, Mastercard Rules

description: Indicates whether the transaction was processed using contactless NFC technology (tap-to-pay). Contactless transactions may qualify for specific interchange categories.
Timestamp when this interchange qualification record was first created in the data platform. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.

Indicates whether the transaction crossed international borders (acquiring country differs from issuing country). Cross-border transactions typically incur higher interchange rates and additional scheme fees.
Result code from CVV verification performed during authorization. CVV match is a qualifying criterion for card-not-present transactions to achieve optimal interchange rates.

Code indicating why the transaction was downgraded to a higher interchange rate category. Null if transaction qualified for optimal rate. Common reasons include missing AVS, non-EMV, delayed settlement, or missing Level 2/3 data.
Human-readable description of why the transaction was downgraded, providing detail on the specific qualification criteria that were not met.

Indicates whether the transaction is subject to Durbin Amendment interchange rate caps (applies to debit cards issued by banks with $10B+ in assets in the United States). Durbin-regulated transactions have capped interchange rates.
Indicates whether the transaction was processed using EMV chip technology. EMV transactions typically qualify for preferential interchange rates due to enhanced security.
The total calculated interchange fee amount for this transaction based on the qualified IRF rate and transaction amount. This is the fee paid from the acquiring bank to the issuing bank.
The fixed fee component of the qualified interchange fee applied to the transaction. Expressed in the settlement currency.
reference: Visa Interchange Reimbursement Fees, Mastercard Interchange Programs

description: The percentage rate component of the qualified interchange fee applied to the transaction amount. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0175 for 1.75%).
reference: Visa Interchange Reimbursement Fees, Mastercard Interchange Programs

description: Indicates whether Level 2 enhanced data (tax amount, customer code, etc.) was provided with the transaction. Level 2 data is required for commercial card interchange optimization.
reference: Visa Commercial Solutions, Mastercard Business Solutions

description: Indicates whether Level 3 line-item data (item descriptions, quantities, unit costs) was provided with the transaction. Level 3 data is required for optimal commercial and purchasing card interchange rates.
reference: Visa Commercial Solutions, Mastercard Business Solutions

description: Status indicating whether the transaction qualified for the optimal interchange rate, was downgraded to a higher-cost tier, is exempt from interchange, or encountered an error during qualification.
Timestamp when the interchange qualification process was executed and the IRF category was determined. Represents the business event time of qualification.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the interchange amount is settled between acquiring and issuing banks.
Date on which the transaction was settled and interchange fees were calculated and transferred between acquiring and issuing banks. Delayed settlement can result in interchange downgrades.

Indicates whether the transaction was authenticated using 3-D Secure protocol (Verified by Visa, Mastercard SecureCode). 3DS authentication is a qualifying criterion for e-commerce interchange optimization.
reference: EMVCo 3-D Secure Specifications, PSD2 SCA Requirements

description: The gross transaction amount on which the interchange fee was calculated. Expressed in the transaction currency before any currency conversion.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the original transaction amount. May differ from settlement currency if cross-border transaction.
Timestamp when this interchange qualification record was last modified. Audit field for data lineage and compliance tracking.
Unique identifier for the card scheme invoice record. Primary key for the scheme invoice entity.
Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Invoice generation requires linking to the card scheme for correct fee line items.

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for audit trail linking each invoice creation to the responsible employee, needed for PCI DSS and internal compliance reporting.
Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: Regulatory reporting and reconciliation require associating a scheme invoice with the dispute case that challenges its amounts.
Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Required for GL posting of scheme invoices; financial reporting mandates linking each invoice to its GL account.
Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Scheme invoices may be issued in various currencies; linking to the FX rate enables accurate accounting and audit of converted totals.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requires each scheme invoice to reference the governing regulatory obligation for audit and compliance verification.
Foreign key linking to settlement.cycle. Business justification: Links scheme invoices to the settlement cycle they belong to, supporting regulatory reporting of scheme fees per cycle.

The net amount of credits, refunds, or additional charges applied to this invoice as adjustments from prior billing periods or corrections. Positive values represent additional charges; negative values represent credits.

The portion of the invoice representing assessment fees charged by the card scheme based on transaction volume or value. Typically calculated as a percentage of Total Payment Volume (TPV) or Gross Merchandise Value (GMV).
The last date of the billing period covered by this scheme invoice. Defines the end of the time window for which fees and assessments are being charged.
The first date of the billing period covered by this scheme invoice. Defines the beginning of the time window for which fees and assessments are being charged.

The portion of the invoice representing fees charged by the card scheme for processing chargebacks and disputes during the billing period. Includes fees for dispute lifecycle management and arbitration services.

The portion of the invoice representing penalties or fines assessed by the card scheme for non-compliance with network rules, PCI DSS requirements, or other regulatory standards. Zero if no penalties were assessed.
reference: PCI Security Standards Council, Card Scheme Compliance Rules

description: The cost center or business unit code responsible for this scheme invoice expense. Used for internal cost allocation and management reporting.
The timestamp when this scheme invoice record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
The portion of the invoice representing fees charged for cross-border or international transactions where the issuing bank and acquiring bank are in different countries or regions.
The date on which this invoice was formally disputed with the card scheme. Null if invoice has not been disputed.
The reason code provided if this invoice is disputed. Indicates the nature of the dispute (e.g., incorrect fee calculation, unrecognized charges, billing error). Null if invoice is not disputed.
The date on which the dispute for this invoice was resolved. Null if dispute is still open or if invoice was never disputed.
The date by which payment must be received by the card scheme to avoid late fees or penalties. Defines the payment deadline for this invoice.

The portion of the invoice representing Interchange Reimbursement Fees (IRF) paid between acquiring and issuing banks through the card scheme. This is the fee paid by the acquiring bank to the issuing bank for each transaction.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this scheme invoice is denominated. All monetary amounts on this invoice are expressed in this currency.
The date on which the card scheme issued this invoice. This is the principal business event timestamp representing when the billing statement was generated and sent.
The URL or file path to the original invoice document (PDF or other format) provided by the card scheme. Used for audit trail and detailed line-item review.

The externally-known unique invoice number assigned by the card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) for this billing statement. This is the business identifier used for reconciliation and payment reference.
Current lifecycle status of the scheme invoice indicating its payment and processing state. Tracks the invoice from issuance through payment completion or dispute resolution.
The portion of the invoice representing fees charged for participation in the card scheme network. This is a recurring membership or access fee for being part of the payment network.
Free-text notes or comments related to this scheme invoice, including special instructions, dispute details, or reconciliation observations. Used for operational communication and audit trail.
The actual date on which payment was made to the card scheme for this invoice. Null if invoice remains unpaid.
The payment instrument or method used to settle this scheme invoice. Indicates how the payment was made to the card scheme.
The unique reference number or transaction identifier for the payment made to settle this invoice. Used for payment reconciliation and audit trail. Null if invoice is unpaid.
The reference identifier linking this scheme invoice to the internal reconciliation record or general ledger entry. Used to track the invoice through the accounts payable and financial close process.
The current status of the reconciliation process for this scheme invoice, indicating whether the invoice has been matched to internal transaction records and general ledger entries.
The portion of the invoice representing fees for specific card scheme services such as tokenization, fraud prevention, dispute resolution, or other value-added services provided by the network.
The total amount of taxes (VAT, GST, sales tax, or other applicable taxes) applied to this scheme invoice. Zero if no taxes are applicable.
The total amount payable to the card scheme for this invoice, representing the sum of all line-item fees, assessments, and charges. This is the net total amount due before any adjustments or credits.

The total monetary value of all transactions processed through the card scheme network during the billing period, expressed in the invoice currency. Represents the Total Payment Volume (TPV) or Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) for fee calculation purposes.
The total number of transactions processed through the card scheme network during the billing period covered by this invoice. Used to validate volume-based fee calculations.
The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this scheme invoice record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
The timestamp when this scheme invoice record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Unique identifier for the scheme invoice line item. Primary key for this entity.
User ID of the finance team member who approved this fee line for payment. Null until approval is granted. Used for segregation of duties controls and audit trail.
reference: SOX Internal Controls, Internal Audit Standards

description: Reference to the dispute case if this fee line is under dispute with the card scheme. Links to the dispute management system for tracking dispute lifecycle, evidence submission, and resolution. Null when no dispute exists.
reference: Internal Dispute Management Standards, Card Scheme Dispute Procedures

description: User ID of the finance team member who approved this fee line for payment. Null until approval is granted. Used for segregation of duties controls and audit trail.
reference: SOX Internal Controls, Internal Audit Standards

description: Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Each fee line must be posted to a specific GL account for accurate financial statements and audit trails.

Reference to the original scheme_invoice_line_id that this adjustment line is correcting. Null for original fee charges. Used to trace adjustment history and maintain audit trail for billing corrections.
reference: SOX Financial Reporting Standards, Internal Audit Standards

description: Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_fee_table. Business justification: scheme_invoice_line details a fee defined in scheme_fee_table; adds scheme_fee_table_id FK
Reference to the parent scheme invoice that this line item belongs to. Links line-level detail to the invoice header for reconciliation and aggregation.

Indicates whether this line item represents an adjustment or correction to a previously billed fee. True for adjustments, credits, or corrections; false for original fee charges. Used to track billing accuracy and dispute resolution.

Code indicating the reason for an adjustment when adjustment_indicator is true. Examples include billing error correction, rate change retroactive application, volume correction, or dispute resolution credit. Null for non-adjustment lines.
reference: Internal Finance Standards, Card Scheme Billing Guidelines

description: Date and time when this fee line was approved for payment by the finance team. Null until approval is granted. Used for payment processing workflow and SOX audit trail.
reference: SOX Internal Controls, Internal Finance Standards

description: Method by which transactions were authorized. EMV chip, contactless NFC, magnetic stripe, manual key entry, ecommerce, recurring billing, or mail-order/telephone-order (MOTO). Interchange rates and fraud liability vary by authorization method. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: chip|contactless|magnetic_stripe|manual|ecommerce|recurring|moto — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: EMVCo Standards, PCI DSS, Visa Core Rules

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this fee is billed. All monetary amounts on this line (transaction_value_basis, fee_amount) are expressed in this currency.
Upper bound of the BIN or IIN range that this fee line applies to. Used in conjunction with bin_range_low to define the card portfolio segment for this fee calculation.
reference: ISO/IEC 7812, EMVCo Standards, Visa Core Rules

description: Lower bound of the BIN or IIN (Issuer Identification Number) range that this fee line applies to. Used for BIN-level interchange qualification and fee segmentation. First 6-8 digits of the card number identifying the issuing bank.
reference: ISO/IEC 7812, EMVCo Standards, Visa Core Rules

description: Classification of the card product that this fee applies to. Interchange rates and scheme fees vary significantly by card product type. Examples include consumer credit, consumer debit, commercial cards, and premium card tiers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit|debit|prepaid|commercial|rewards|signature|platinum|world|world_elite — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Visa Product and Service Rules, Mastercard Standards, EMVCo

description: Internal cost center or business unit code for allocating this fee expense. Used for management accounting, profitability analysis, and internal chargeback to business lines or product portfolios.
reference: Internal Finance Standards, ERP Cost Accounting

description: Date and time when this scheme invoice line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Audit Standards, Internal Data Governance

description: Indicates whether this fee line applies to cross-border transactions (issuer and acquirer in different countries). Cross-border transactions typically incur additional scheme fees and higher interchange rates.
reference: Visa Core Rules, Mastercard Rules, PSD2

description: Indicates whether this fee line applies to transactions subject to Durbin Amendment interchange caps (US Regulation II). Durbin-regulated debit transactions from large issuers have capped interchange rates. Critical for US market compliance and revenue recognition.
reference: Federal Reserve Regulation II (Durbin Amendment), Visa Core Rules

description: Exchange rate applied to convert settlement_currency_code amounts to billing_currency_code amounts. Null when billing and settlement currencies are the same. Used for cross-border fee reconciliation and dynamic currency conversion (DCC) scenarios.

Calculated fee amount for this line item in the billing currency. Represents the actual charge from the card scheme for this specific fee component. This is the primary financial value used for reconciliation and accounts payable processing.

High-level classification of the fee for reporting and cost allocation. Groups related fee types into business-meaningful categories for financial analysis and revenue recognition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: interchange|assessment|network|authorization|cross_border|chargeback|dispute|service|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Internal Finance Standards, Card Scheme Billing Guidelines

description: Human-readable description of the fee type and its business purpose. Provides context for the fee_type_code, explaining what service or transaction activity triggered this charge.
reference: Visa Product and Service Rules, Mastercard Standards

description: The rate applied to calculate this fee. May represent a percentage (e.g., 0.015 for 1.5%), a per-transaction amount, or a tiered rate depending on fee_rate_type. Used in conjunction with transaction_volume_basis or transaction_value_basis to compute fee_amount.
reference: Visa Product and Service Rules, Mastercard Standards

description: Indicates how the fee_rate should be interpreted and applied. Percentage rates are applied to transaction_value_basis, per-transaction rates are multiplied by transaction_volume_basis, tiered rates vary by volume thresholds, flat rates are fixed amounts, and basis points represent hundredths of a percent.
reference: Internal Finance Standards, Card Scheme Billing Guidelines

description: Card scheme-specific code identifying the type of fee being charged. Examples include interchange reimbursement fees (IRF), assessment fees, network fees, authorization fees, cross-border fees, and scheme service fees. Aligns with Visa and Mastercard fee code taxonomies.
reference: Visa Core Rules, Mastercard Rules, ISO 20022

description: Specific interchange qualification tier or program that this fee line is associated with. Examples include CPS/Retail, CPS/Card Not Present, EIRF, Standard, or scheme-specific tier names. Determines the applicable interchange reimbursement fee (IRF) rate.
reference: Visa Product and Service Rules, Mastercard Standards

description: Sequential line number within the parent scheme invoice. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items in reconciliation and dispute processes.

Four-digit ISO 18245 code classifying the type of merchant or business where transactions occurred. Interchange qualification and fee structures often vary by MCC. Used for industry-specific fee segmentation.
reference: ISO 18245, Visa Core Rules, Mastercard Rules

description: Free-text field for additional comments, explanations, or context about this fee line. Used by finance and operations teams to document unusual circumstances, reconciliation findings, or dispute rationale.
Reference number of the payment transaction used to settle this fee line with the card scheme. Links to accounts payable payment records and bank statements. Null until payment is executed.
reference: SWIFT Standards, ACH Rules, Internal Treasury Standards

description: Current status of the reconciliation process for this fee line. Tracks whether the scheme-billed fee has been matched against internal transaction records, whether variances exist, and whether the line has been approved for payment.
reference: SOX Internal Controls, Internal Audit Standards

description: End date of the service period that this fee line covers. Defines the end of the transaction activity window used to calculate this fee. Used for period-based reconciliation and revenue recognition cutoff.
reference: SOX Financial Reporting Standards, Internal Billing Standards

description: Start date of the service period that this fee line covers. Defines the beginning of the transaction activity window used to calculate this fee. Used for period-based reconciliation and accrual accounting.
reference: SOX Financial Reporting Standards, Internal Billing Standards

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the underlying transactions were settled. May differ from billing_currency_code when cross-border or multi-currency processing is involved.

Type of transaction activity that generated this fee. Different transaction types may incur different fee structures and rates. Critical for accurate fee reconciliation and revenue recognition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase|cash_advance|refund|reversal|chargeback|fee|adjustment — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ISO 8583, Visa Core Rules, Mastercard Rules

description: Total monetary value of transactions that this fee line is calculated against. Represents the dollar volume basis used for percentage-based fee calculations. Expressed in the billing currency.

Number of transactions or events that this fee line is calculated against. Represents the volume basis (transaction count, authorization count, or other countable unit) used to compute the fee amount.
Date and time when this scheme invoice line record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.
reference: SOX Audit Standards, Internal Data Governance

description: Difference between the scheme-billed fee_amount and the internally calculated expected fee amount. Positive values indicate overbilling, negative values indicate underbilling. Null when reconciliation_status is matched. Used for dispute initiation and billing accuracy monitoring.
reference: SOX Internal Controls, Internal Finance Standards

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the acquiring agreement record.
Reference to the stored document that contains the amendment text.
Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Contracts specify settlement currency; linking to the currency reference ensures legal clarity and correct settlement processing.
Reference to the specific compliance rule set governing this agreement.

Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Business need: Acquiring agreements bind a merchant to an acquiring partner (bank). Required for settlement reporting, regulatory compliance, and partner performance metrics.
Unique identifier of the merchant party to which the agreement applies.
Reference to the pricing plan associated with this agreement.
Identifier of the bank account where settlement funds are transferred.
Current lifecycle state of the agreement.
