reference: Merchant Agreement Termination Clause, Record Retention Policy

description: The maximum amount (in processing currency) allowed for a single transaction. Transactions exceeding this limit are declined at authorization. Set based on merchant risk profile and business model.
reference: Risk Management Policy, Authorization Rules Engine

description: The timestamp when this merchant record was last modified. Updated automatically on any field change. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.
The primary website URL of the merchant business. Used for business verification, fraud screening, and customer service reference. Required for e-commerce merchants.
reference: PCI DSS Requirement 6.6, Merchant Verification Standards

description: Unique identifier for the merchant contact record. Primary key.
Reference to the merchant entity this contact is associated with. Links contact to the parent merchant record for relationship management.
Indicates whether this contact should receive chargeback and dispute notifications. Enables targeted communication for time-sensitive dispute response requirements.
reference: Visa Core Rules, Mastercard Rules, Dispute Management Best Practices

description: Indicates whether this contact is authorized to receive communications containing Payment Card Industry (PCI) sensitive data. Ensures PCI DSS compliance in data transmission.
Indicates whether this contact should receive settlement and funding reports. Used for financial reconciliation and treasury management communications.
reference: ERP - Oracle Financials, Settlement Best Practices

description: Current lifecycle status of the merchant contact record. Determines whether the contact should receive communications and appear in active contact lists.
Classification of the contact role within the merchant organization. Determines the purpose and scope of communication with this contact.
User identifier or system account that created this contact record. Supports audit trail and accountability for data governance.
Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data lineage and compliance reporting.
Business unit or department within the merchant organization where the contact works. Used for routing communications to appropriate functional areas.
Date when this contact record became active and valid for the merchant relationship. Supports temporal tracking and audit requirements.
Date when this contact record ceased to be active for the merchant relationship. Null for currently active contacts. Supports historical tracking and compliance audit trails.
Primary email address for electronic communication with the merchant contact. Used for transaction notifications, dispute correspondence, and operational alerts.
Indicates whether the contact has opted out of marketing or non-essential email communications. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.
Facsimile number for document transmission. Used for formal dispute documentation and regulatory correspondence requiring written records.
Given name of the merchant contact person. Used for personalized communication and identification.
Indicates whether this contact has legal authority to sign contracts and agreements on behalf of the merchant. Critical for KYC and contract execution workflows.
Indicates whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the merchant. Used to prioritize communication routing and escalation paths.
Professional title or position of the contact within the merchant organization. Indicates authority level and functional responsibility.
Date when the current KYC verification expires and re-verification is required. Supports periodic review requirements under AML and CTF regulations.
Date when KYC verification was completed for this contact. Used to track verification currency and trigger re-verification workflows per regulatory requirements.
Status of identity verification and KYC checks for this contact. Required for compliance with AML and CTF regulations for authorized signers and key contacts.
reference: KYC Standards, AML Regulations, BSA, FATF

description: Date of the most recent communication or interaction with this contact. Used for relationship management and contact data quality maintenance.
User identifier or system account that last modified this contact record. Supports audit trail and accountability for data changes.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this contact record. Critical for change tracking, audit trails, and data synchronization.
Family name or surname of the merchant contact person. Used for formal identification and communication.
Middle name or initial of the merchant contact person. Optional field for complete name representation.
Mobile or cellular phone number for SMS notifications and mobile communication. Used for time-sensitive alerts and two-factor authentication.
Free-form text field for additional information, special instructions, or relationship notes about the contact. Supports relationship management and customer service quality.
Internal extension number for direct routing within the merchant organization phone system. Enables direct contact bypass of main switchboard.
Primary telephone number for voice communication with the merchant contact. Used for urgent matters, dispute resolution, and relationship management.
Contact's preferred method for receiving communications from the payment processor. Ensures compliance with communication preferences and improves response rates.
reference: GDPR Article 21, CCPA, CRM Best Practices

description: ISO 639-2 three-letter language code indicating the contact's preferred language for communication. Supports multilingual merchant base and regulatory compliance in international markets.
Alternate email address for backup communication. Used when primary email is unavailable or for redundant critical notifications.
Indicates whether the contact has opted out of SMS or text message communications. Required for compliance with telecommunications regulations and privacy laws.
Identifier of the operational system that originated this contact record. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system integration scenarios.
reference: Data Governance Framework, ETL Best Practices

description: Unique identifier of this contact in the source operational system. Enables cross-system reconciliation and bidirectional data synchronization.
reference: Data Governance Framework, ETL Best Practices

description: Explanation or reason code for the current contact status. Provides audit trail for status changes and supports compliance documentation.
IANA time zone identifier for the contact's location. Used for scheduling communications during business hours and timestamping interactions appropriately.

The trade name or 'Doing Business As' name under which this location operates, if different from the legal merchant name.

Indicates whether real-time fraud detection and prevention monitoring is enabled for transactions at this location.

description: Status of Know Your Customer (KYC) and Know Your Business (KYB) verification for this location: verified, pending review, failed verification, or expired requiring re-verification.

description: Date of the most recent transaction processed at this location, used for dormancy monitoring and lifecycle management.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the merchant location in decimal degrees for mapping and proximity analytics.
Business name or identifier for this specific merchant location (e.g., 'Downtown Store', 'Airport Terminal 3').

Current operational status of the merchant location in its lifecycle: active (processing transactions), inactive (temporarily not processing), suspended (compliance hold), pending (onboarding), or closed (permanently offboarded).
Classification of the merchant location type: physical storefront, kiosk, online, mobile Point of Sale (mPOS), virtual, or pop-up.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the merchant location in decimal degrees for mapping and proximity analytics.
Four-digit Merchant Category Code (MCC) classification for this location, which may override the parent merchant MCC for location-specific business activities.
Unique Merchant Identification Number (MID) assigned to this location by the acquiring bank or payment processor for transaction routing and settlement.
Date when this merchant location was successfully onboarded and provisioned in the payment processing system.
Standard operating hours for the location (e.g., 'Mon-Fri 9AM-5PM, Sat 10AM-2PM') used for customer service and transaction pattern analysis.

business_glossary_term: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) Compliance Level
description: PCI DSS compliance level assigned to this location based on annual transaction volume: Level 1 (>6M), Level 2 (1M-6M), Level 3 (20K-1M), Level 4 (<20K).
reference: PCI Security Standards Council PCI DSS

State, province, or administrative region of the merchant location.

IANA timezone identifier for the merchant location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London) used for transaction timestamp normalization.

Unique identifier for the MCC assignment record. Primary key for the MCC assignment entity.

Identifier of the user or system operator who created or approved this MCC assignment. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Identifier of the user or system operator who last modified this MCC assignment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Reference to the merchant to whom this MCC is assigned. Links to the merchant master record.
Reference to the specific merchant location or outlet for which this MCC is assigned. Nullable if MCC applies to the entire merchant entity rather than a specific location.
Identifier of the user or system operator who created or approved this MCC assignment. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Identifier of the user or system operator who last modified this MCC assignment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp when the MCC assignment was approved by the designated approver. Nullable if approval is not required or has not yet occurred.

Entity that assigned or approved the MCC. Can be a card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) or the acquiring bank/payment facilitator. Determines the authoritative source for the MCC classification.
Date on which the MCC was assigned to the merchant or merchant location. Represents the business event date of the assignment decision.

Free-text explanation or business justification for the MCC assignment. May include details about the merchant's primary business activity, product mix, or regulatory considerations that influenced the MCC selection.

Current lifecycle status of the MCC assignment. Active assignments are used for transaction processing; pending assignments await approval; suspended or revoked assignments are temporarily or permanently disabled.

Categorized reason for changing the MCC from a previous assignment. Applicable only when previous_mcc_code is populated. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting. Timestamp when this MCC assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

Date from which the MCC assignment becomes active and is used for transaction processing, interchange qualification, and reporting. May differ from assignment_date if there is a planned future activation.
Date on which the MCC assignment expires or is no longer valid. Nullable for open-ended assignments. Used to track MCC changes and historical assignments.
Indicates the impact of this MCC assignment on interchange rate qualification. Certain MCCs qualify for enhanced or premium interchange rates, while others may result in downgrades or restrictions.

description: Timestamp when this MCC assignment record was last updated or modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

Four-digit code that classifies the merchant's type of business. Determines interchange rates, spending category analytics, and fraud risk profiling. Assigned by card schemes (Visa, Mastercard) or acquirer.
Human-readable description of the MCC code, describing the business category (e.g., 'Grocery Stores and Supermarkets', 'Airlines', 'Hotels and Motels').
Additional free-text notes or comments related to this MCC assignment. May include operational details, special instructions, or historical context.

The MCC code that was previously assigned to this merchant or location before the current assignment. Nullable for initial assignments. Used to track MCC change history and analyze reclassification patterns.
Regulatory or compliance reporting category associated with this MCC. Used for AML/CTF reporting, tax reporting (e.g., 1099-K thresholds), and industry-specific regulatory requirements.
Risk classification associated with this MCC for fraud detection and prevention purposes. High-risk MCCs (e.g., gambling, adult entertainment, travel) trigger enhanced monitoring and fraud rules.
Date on which the card scheme approved this MCC assignment. Nullable if scheme approval is not required or has not yet been obtained.
Reference number or case ID provided by the card scheme when approving this MCC assignment. Used for audit trail and dispute resolution.
Indicates whether this MCC assignment requires explicit approval from the card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, etc.) before it can be activated. True for high-risk or restricted MCCs.
Name or identifier of the operational system from which this MCC assignment record originated (e.g., Merchant Management System, CRM, card scheme portal).
Unique identifier of this MCC assignment record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.

Unique identifier for the pricing plan. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Pricing plans are defined per currency; referencing the currency master ensures consistent pricing and compliance with local regulations.

business_glossary_term: Automated Clearing House (ACH) Transaction Fee Amount
description: Fee charged per ACH (bank transfer) transaction processed for the merchant, expressed in the plan's base currency. Typically lower than card transaction fees.
reference: NACHA ACH Network Rules; Payment Processing Platform

description: Separate MDR percentage applied specifically to American Express card transactions, which often have different pricing than Visa/Mastercard, expressed as a decimal.
reference: American Express Merchant Agreement; Card Scheme Rules

description: Fee charged per transaction when Address Verification System (AVS) checks are performed, expressed in the plan's base currency. Helps reduce fraud risk for card-not-present transactions.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Fraud Detection Platform

description: Fee charged per settlement batch submitted by the merchant, expressed in the plan's base currency. Applies to merchants using batch settlement rather than real-time capture.
reference: Transaction Processing Platform; Merchant Service Agreement

description: Fee charged to the merchant for each chargeback dispute, regardless of outcome, expressed in the plan's base currency. Covers administrative and processing costs.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Dispute and Chargeback Management System

description: The minimum contract duration in months that the merchant must commit to when assigned this pricing plan. Null for month-to-month plans.
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Contract Management Standards

description: Identifier of the system user or administrator who created this pricing plan record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
reference: Internal Data Management Standard; SOX Compliance

description: The date and time when this pricing plan record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
Markup percentage applied to the foreign exchange (FX) rate when Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) is offered to cardholders, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0300 for 3.00% above spot rate).
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; FX and DCC Standards

description: MDR percentage applied specifically to debit card transactions, typically lower than credit card rates due to Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) caps, expressed as a decimal.
reference: Federal Reserve Regulation II; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: Penalty fee charged if the merchant terminates the agreement before the contract term expires, expressed in the plan's base currency. Null if no early termination penalty applies.
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Contract Management Standards

description: The date from which this pricing plan becomes binding and applicable to assigned merchants. Transactions on or after this date are governed by this plan's terms.
The date on which this pricing plan ceases to be valid. Nullable for open-ended plans. Transactions after this date require a new plan assignment.
The markup percentage added on top of the actual interchange cost in interchange-plus pricing models (e.g., 0.0050 for 0.50% above interchange). Null for non-interchange-plus models.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Merchant Service Agreement

description: Indicates whether actual interchange fees (IRF) are passed through to the merchant at cost (true) or absorbed into a blended rate (false). True for interchange-plus and cost-plus models.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Interchange Reimbursement Fee Standards

description: Additional percentage fee applied to cross-border transactions where the card issuer is in a different country than the merchant, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0100 for 1.00%).
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Cross-Border Payment Standards

description: The base percentage rate charged to the merchant per transaction, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0275 for 2.75%). This is the primary revenue component for flat-rate and blended pricing models.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Merchant Service Agreement

description: Current lifecycle status of the pricing plan. Active plans are available for assignment; inactive plans are no longer offered but may still govern existing merchants; pending plans are under approval; suspended plans are temporarily unavailable; archived plans are historical records only.
reference: Merchant Management System - Pricing Configuration Module

description: The middle MDR percentage tier in tiered pricing models, applied to transactions that partially meet qualification criteria (e.g., keyed-in transactions, rewards cards), expressed as a decimal.
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Tiered Pricing Standards

description: Fixed monthly account maintenance or service fee charged to the merchant, independent of transaction volume, expressed in the plan's base currency.
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Payment Processing Platform

description: The minimum monthly fee the merchant must pay regardless of transaction volume, expressed in the plan's base currency. If monthly processing fees fall below this threshold, the merchant is charged the difference.
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Payment Processing Platform

description: The highest MDR percentage tier in tiered pricing models, applied to transactions that fail qualification criteria (e.g., corporate cards, international cards, manually entered), expressed as a decimal.
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Tiered Pricing Standards

description: Indicates whether this pricing plan is available for assignment to sub-merchants under a Payment Facilitator (PayFac) model. True if eligible for PayFac sub-merchants; false if only for direct merchants.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; PayFac Program Standards

description: Annual or monthly fee charged to merchants for PCI DSS compliance program administration, security scanning, and attestation support, expressed in the plan's base currency.
reference: PCI Security Standards Council; PCI DSS Requirements

description: Monthly penalty fee charged to merchants who fail to maintain PCI DSS compliance, expressed in the plan's base currency. Escalates with duration of non-compliance.
reference: PCI Security Standards Council; PCI DSS Requirements

description: Fixed fee charged per transaction in addition to the percentage-based MDR, expressed in the plan's base currency (e.g., 0.10 for $0.10 per transaction).
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Payment Processing Platform

description: Externally-known unique business identifier for the pricing plan, used in contracts and merchant communications.
reference: Merchant Management System - Pricing Configuration Module

description: Detailed description of the pricing plan, including key features, target merchant segments, and value proposition.
reference: Merchant Management System - Pricing Configuration Module

description: Human-readable name of the pricing plan (e.g., 'Standard Retail', 'Enterprise Plus', 'Startup Flat Rate').
reference: Merchant Management System - Pricing Configuration Module

description: The fundamental pricing structure applied to merchant transactions. Flat-rate charges a single percentage; interchange-plus passes through interchange costs plus a markup; tiered groups transactions into qualified/mid-qualified/non-qualified buckets; blended averages all transaction types; cost-plus adds a fixed margin to actual costs; custom indicates bespoke negotiated terms.
reference: PCI Security Standards Council; Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules

description: The lowest MDR percentage tier in tiered pricing models, applied to transactions that meet all qualification criteria (e.g., swiped consumer credit cards), expressed as a decimal.
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Tiered Pricing Standards

description: Fee charged to the merchant for processing a refund or credit transaction, expressed in the plan's base currency. Some plans waive this fee; others charge per refund.
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Transaction Processing Platform

description: Fee charged to the merchant for each retrieval request (pre-chargeback inquiry) from the issuing bank, expressed in the plan's base currency.
reference: Visa Core Rules; Mastercard Rules; Dispute and Chargeback Management System

description: One-time fee charged when a merchant is onboarded and assigned to this pricing plan, covering account setup, KYC processing, and initial configuration, expressed in the plan's base currency.
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Merchant Management System

description: Fee charged for generating and delivering monthly merchant statements (paper or electronic), expressed in the plan's base currency.
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Payment Processing Platform

description: Identifier of the system user or administrator who last modified this pricing plan record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
reference: Internal Data Management Standard; SOX Compliance

description: The date and time when this pricing plan record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Fee charged when a merchant obtains authorization via phone call to the authorization center rather than electronic authorization, expressed in the plan's base currency.
reference: Payment Gateway and Authorization Engine; Merchant Service Agreement

description: Monthly transaction volume threshold (in base currency) that triggers preferential pricing or tier upgrades. Used in volume-based pricing models where rates decrease as volume increases.
reference: Merchant Service Agreement; Payment Processing Platform

description: System‑generated unique identifier for the chargeback threshold record.
Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Chargeback limits are defined per currency; referencing the master currency enables consistent enforcement across regions.

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the chargeback threshold record.
